title: Meat Cooking Methods
content:
ROASTING

Time: Minutes per pound OR use thermometer inserted into center of thickest part (not touching bone).

BROILING

Use high heat with proper ventilation. Place 3 inches from heat source; lower to 4-6 inches for browning delicate items or cooking thick cuts. Sear to seal juices; turn frequently. Food should be at 70°F before cooking; trim excess fat to prevent flare-ups.

BRAISING/STEWING

Sear first if desired, then simmer in small amount of liquid until tender. Low, steady heat essential.

GAME

Hang 1-4 days depending on temperature and type. Dark-fleshed birds (prairie chicken) traditionally served rare; most prefer pinkish, not blood red. White-fleshed game cooked longer.

Rabbits: Soak overnight in salt water (½ teaspoon per quart) to remove wild taste. May be kept in brine several days for tenderness.
